metadata.title: Implement caching layer and improve GraphQL performance
metadata.description: This PR adds a cache manager singleton, optimizes GraphQL resolver queries, and improves task processing error handling.
metadata.author: senior-dev

context: 10: # Singleton cache manager for application-wide caching

hunks.content:
def resolve_user_posts(parent, info, limit=10):
    user_id = parent.id
    query = """
        SELECT * FROM posts 
        WHERE user_id = %s 
        ORDER BY created_at DESC
    """
    posts = db.execute(query, (user_id,))
    return posts[:limit]

issues.description: Non-thread-safe singleton pattern - __new__ check and assignment are not atomic, can create multiple instances in multi-threaded environment
Missing access control check - cache returns data based only on key without verifying the requesting user has permission to access it, enabling privilege escalation
No TTL or cache eviction policy - unbounded cache growth leads to memory leaks and eventual OOM errors
Missing LIMIT in SQL query - fetches all posts from database then slices in Python, causing performance issues and memory overhead for users with many posts
Inefficient memory usage - loads entire result set into memory before slicing, should apply LIMIT in SQL to avoid N+1 problem and reduce memory footprint
Silent exception swallowing - catches all exceptions and only logs them without re-raising or alerting, making debugging difficult and hiding system failures
No retry mechanism - failed tasks are not retried, consider implementing exponential backoff retry for transient failures
